reviews.review_title: A polished and enjoyable rhythm game
reviews.review_description:
While this game has some downsides, it's all in all a polished game similar to Beat Saber, something which you can use as a daily exercise routine.

There are two parts to the game, a rhythm game and a meta game involving a dancing anime girl or robot.

The rhythm game involves cutting arcs that come towards you as they reach a circle. It can be played lazily by pointing the controller, but it's a lot of fun to move your arm and body with it, and it works well when played like this. The higher the difficulty level, the smaller the arcs can become.

An anime girl / female robot / robot dog dance in the scene while you're playing. I tend to play with the robot dog, because it's less distracting, but I think it's enjoyable to watch the dance moves of the girls. Sometimes the robot gets too close to me while playing, and that can be annoying.

The meta game involves earning 'intimacy points', which gain levels and unlock new stages (backgrounds) and costumes. There are also keys which are used to unlock custom songs (one key per song) or as an alternate way to unlock costumes. There's no real need to give that too much attention, just playing gets these points and keys, and stages and costumes and dance moves unlock automatically.

Graphically the game is really well made, the stages are sharp, and the animations decent, although the robot dog's arms do regularly clip through its head, and it occasionally passes through me, which feels weird.

Would have been nice if more songs were available, even as DLC.

Technical issues:

Long loading time, about 40 seconds of staring at a static anime girl.

The game seems to take too much RAM. Oculus Home restarts on exit, and if the Go goes to sleep, the game reloads when it awakens. This may also be the reason why custom songs sometimes stutter or crash the game.

The song Z_Energy has some problems with the music. Z_Hollah is barely audible.

There's garbage to the right of the head on the song selection screen.

reviews.review_title: Not perfect but a great experience!
reviews.review_description:
Maybe we can't have Beat Saver for Oculus Go but at least we've got Very Star! I love this game even though VIVA's hair move is very unnatural compared to her truly realistic (probably motion captured) dance or the option for touching her body and making her feel embarrassed like sexually is awkward, it still makes me want to dance to the music and I think that's wonderful. I just wish if the developer had made it able to customize VIVA's face parts (like eye colors, nose shapes, hair styles etc.) since her face is too much animetic and kinda childish to me haha.
I see that the developer probably made this game for men especially heterosexuals but I want to say that I'm an asexual girl and yet totally fan of this game<3
Where the sexy, generating, gorgeous guys?

This game is really great. The graphic is awesome the included tracks are also very great. The possibility to use your own MP3 tracks is also great. The only not completely positive thing is the note placement, they don't seem too match with the rhythm. I would ask the developers to work on the Note placement and the game could be a little bit harder in the hardest settings for me. Overall it's a really good game and I'm hoping for future improvements so I Give It 5 Stars.

I love this game but the resolution is low and the aliasing is quite bad, especially in the lounge.  But it runs great on the Go and the S8/S9 at a higher eye buffer resolution so please consider adding a render scale adjustment in an options panel.

reviews.review_title: Cheap and a lot of fun
reviews.review_description:
I had some doubts about a rhythm game using the Oculus Go's controller, but am glad to be proven wrong.  
Makes me feel a little bit of a perv when the scoring is measured in intimacy with a cute anime robot, but I'm big enough, fat enough and ugly enough not to let such things bother me, and since you can switch her for a cute alien robot teddy bear like thing, it even would be fine for younger family members.

What are you waiting for? buy this game! For $3.99 this is an amazing deal for the best rhythm game on mobile VR. Upgrades, Outfits, Free Music, use your own music and even watch a movie with your personal dancer VIA VR storage. Purchase and fall in love now! ;)

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your comments. I will continue to do my best.

reviews.review_description: As a woman who plays VR, the idea of a virtual anime girl dancing to all my songs isn't that appealing.. Not to mention kind of awkward. But luckily the app has a feature at the very start that let's you turn the robot into a fluffy little alien creature! It's so cute and is definitely an app with lots of potential. My only concern is it occasionally crashes when I try playing my own music.

I really appreciate that in this game is not my avatar is  dancing in front of me, but the hot Android anime-style girl is dancing for me

Simple, yet effective game design. Very good and modern electronic music. Very fits in genre and VR. My favorite Gear VR game for now

reviews.review_title: Closest thing to Project DIVA on Oculus
reviews.review_description:
While this is obviously a more modest creation, it clearly draws inspiration from the Hatsune Miku: Project DIVA series. It even features a fully functioning DIVA room, where you listen to your own music or watch movies saved in internal storage. 
There are 2 ways to play the rhythm game - with button presses and 'swipes' or the newly added 'slash' mode, which is easier, and arguably more fun. A handful of tunes are included, or you can add your own - although the rhythm of music you add doesn't really match to the onscreen prompts. Quite a few costumes to unlock, including some Drossel Von Flugel-inspired gynoid outfits. One of the better rhythm games on Oculus Go, with plenty of anime-influenced cuteness, and sexy, gyrating robot girls.

So I have played this game now for more then 4 hours and still love it!!! I love the way the game is setup and I can’t wait to get my hands on those Angel Mecha Suits.

The controls do work a bit different then expected but these are explained well in the tutorial (which I forgot to read in the beginning). 

You got 4 playable modes. I do recommend Crazy mode even though the dancing girl can be quite distracting ;-)

Hope a world wide leadership board will be added I would love to see my score compared to other players!!! 

Keep up the good work developers!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your suggestion, I will add the international leaderboard as soon as possible.

reviews.review_description: This is a treat for the eyes and ears. Beautiful, over the top neon graphics combined with thumping tunes is always going to be a winner. The game itself has a nice learning curve, however, some of the combos are really tricky to pull off. The ability to use your own music is also a real plus. Super fun and well worth the asking price

developer_response.body: Thank you very much, I will try to add some new game modes in the future. If you have good suggestions, please feel free to give me feedback.

reviews.review_description: This app is far from the most stable app. It tends to crash rather often particularly if you have a large audio library. I'd recommend that you saved the songs that you like and make sure to click the back to the home button in the app then close the app entirely and then reopen the app to make sure the love list saves.

Like anime version of Beat Saber! 
However it seems the detection on the left edge (I use right hand) is not that responsive. Maybe some adjustment needed for right or left hand?

reviews.review_title: Can’t get past menus Oculus Go
reviews.review_description: Yes/ No choices can’t get past when the pointer won’t focus on the choice.
UPDATE: Will the s6 series version get updated with new content again like vivas room and clothes?

developer_response.body: Sorry for my friend, the 1.2 update is mainly on the screen effect. S6 runs will be stuck. So this upgrade is not included. :-(

reviews.review_description: So this is basically a rhythm game (like osu!) and for what it is, it's pretty fun, especially because you can add your own music. The atmosphere is really nice as well. With the points you get from playing the game you can buy the avatar new outfits. I'd love if the creators would add some more customization options, at least a new hair color would be neat. I also really need an option where you can set the sound effect volume. Sometimes the sound effects are way louder than the music and that can be irritating.
Always fun to play.

This has actually become one of my favorite Oculus Go games; it is a really nice touch that you can add your own music, and the VIVA room is kinda like a VIP lounge in the club. My only complaints are that there are not very many arenas to dance in,  and the sound effects are not volume adjustable. There really needs to be a settings area where you can change the volume of the voices and sounds within the game, so that when you are rockin the music loud you don't blast yourself with the annoying little blings and blangs and irritating talk show host voice screaming "LEVEL ONE",etc. It would be a 5 star game for me if these two small flaws were addressed.

Fantastic rhythm game and dynamic mode makes it a great single hand beat Sabre game. Love the fact you can play your own music in the game but hate the fact the longer songs cut off mid-way while I'm still enjoying it. Another thing the devs could add is a different range of characters for different styles of music, for example I would much prefer an avatar that plays guitar or rocks out when I'm playing my rock metal music than the current ones. Overall great game 5 stars.
